assembledPieces.role: Compresses the repeated endpoint availability split/handoff pattern into the current finite split discipline.
assembledPieces.boundary: Leaves the p4217 unavailable complement open and does not prove global termination.

assembledPieces.role: Records the typed frontier-ledger transition/rank discipline for the current ledger cases.
assembledPieces.boundary: Rank is refined for current transitions, not a global all-N decreasing measure.

assembledPieces.role: Stops the q193..q389 finite q-cover staircase from expanding without a real theorem object.
assembledPieces.boundary: A naked larger q-cover or singleton q-child descent is forbidden.

assembledPieces.role: Audits the row-uniform two-root q-cover transition and records that the surface grows rather than decreases.
assembledPieces.boundary: The two-root law is not by itself a well-founded measure or finite cover.

assembledPieces.role: Audits candidate p4217 complement structural surfaces and blocks selector-ladder/q-cover reuse.
assembledPieces.boundary: No repo-owned p4217 complement cover, impossibility theorem, or decreasing invariant is currently available.

assembledPieces.role: Blocks promotion of bounded CRT replay to all-future recurrence without a source theorem/generator.
assembledPieces.boundary: No local all-future recurrence, finite-Q partition, or restored generator is present.

assembledPieces.role: Audits local frontier-engine/code/output source surfaces and prepares the exact theorem wedge.
assembledPieces.boundary: Finds finite search/menu evidence but no local universal theorem.

assembledPieces.role: Decides the prepared wedge as a blocker: the guarded live/planning result supplies no theorem evidence.
assembledPieces.boundary: The finite 280-row menu and incomplete ORP lane cannot be promoted to an all-N proof.

assembledPieces.role: Binds the synthetic 282/841 representative to a live family-menu row and witness square.
assembledPieces.boundary: Useful for recombination, but not a standalone all-N proof.

assembledPieces.role: Restores finite row-menu chronology for the observed first 282 failure.
assembledPieces.boundary: Replay is finite unless joined to an all-future recurrence/source theorem.

assembledPieces.role: Audits compact endpoint evidence against the top repair-class/mod-50 boundary.
assembledPieces.boundary: Compact 1..40500 evidence is not a public raw all-N proof and cannot justify 40501+ rollout by itself.

whyNotGlobalConvergence: The q-cover lane has a nonconvergence blocker and the row-uniform two-root transition grows the q-avoiding class surface.
The p4217 complement has exact local parameterizations and blockers but no cover, impossibility theorem, or global decreasing invariant.
The mod-50 bounded replay lane has finite evidence only: the richest menu has 280 rows and no restored all-future source theorem.
The finite repair witness set is not closed by the tuple square-prime pool; outside tuple-pool primes are 5, 67, 211, 257.
The guarded ORP theorem-wedge result produced no theorem text and cannot be promoted to proof evidence.

residualTheoremAtoms.neededTheorem: Every residue in the p4217 unavailable complement is impossible, covered by a finite selector family, or covered by a structural square-obstruction decomposition with a well-founded rank.
residualTheoremAtoms.currentBoundary: The structural complement invariant blocker found no repo-owned invariant, and q-cover/singleton selector ladders are paused.

residualTheoremAtoms.neededTheorem: A symbolic recurrence/source generator for every future mod-50 relevant-pair row, or a finite Q partition that covers all future square witnesses.
residualTheoremAtoms.currentBoundary: The source-theorem blocker, source archaeology wedge, and theorem-wedge decision blocker all fail to restore this object.

residualTheoremAtoms.neededTheorem: Future repair rows cannot introduce square-witness primes outside a finite audited pool, or the pool must be enlarged by a proved finite partition.
residualTheoremAtoms.currentBoundary: The current finite menu already has outside tuple-pool repair witness primes 5, 67, 211, 257, so tuple rows alone do not close Q.

residualTheoremAtoms.neededTheorem: A theorem proving the compact 1..40500 endpoint boundary suffices for every later row, or an audited external threshold bridge accepted into repo-owned proof scope.
residualTheoremAtoms.currentBoundary: The compact endpoint packet is evidence, not a raw all-N scan or a 40501+ rollout license.

residualTheoremAtoms.neededTheorem: A final recombination theorem joining endpoint staircase, frontier ledger, complement cover, 282/841 chronology, mod-50 recurrence/finite-Q closure, and endpoint sufficiency.
residualTheoremAtoms.currentBoundary: The current packet only assembles the residual; it does not discharge the residual atoms.

selectionReason: The mod-50 source/theorem wedge has just been exhausted locally and by one guarded incomplete live run.
The finite q-cover lane is blocked as nondecreasing.
The p4217 unavailable complement remains the largest exact residual with an existing parameterized surface and an allowed bulk-cover/impossibility route.

oneNextAction.action: Attempt a theorem-facing p4217 unavailable-complement cover or impossibility theorem from the assembled residual, using a bulk/structural/ranked argument rather than singleton fallback selectors.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: If no complement cover/impossibility can be proved locally, emit a sharper formal recombination blocker naming the missing p4217 theorem object and preserving the q-cover/mod-50 blocks.

proofBoundary: This packet is a recombination residual assembly, not an all-N proof. It names the theorem atoms still outside the proof after the mod-50 theorem-wedge blocker and keeps finite q-cover/selector expansion blocked until a new theorem object is produced.
